title: Treubella macrocarpa
intro: Treubella macrocarpa, also known as the Brazilian cherry tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae, native to the Amazon rainforest. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Treubella macrocarpa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in topical applications for wound healing and skin conditions.
